keywords.serpWeakness: Dominated by big paint brands (Sherwin-Williams, Behr, Benjamin Moore) with calculators buried in store-locator UX, heavy scripts, and forced mobile redirects. Older independent sites lack modern UI and accessibility.
userComplaints: Forces location/store selection before showing estimate
Slow loading because of heavy trackers and ad scripts

keywords.keyword: how much paint do i need
keywords.serpWeakness: Most top results are blog posts with generic formulas, not interactive tools. The few calculators require step-by-step forms and no visual feedback.
userComplaints: Results are vague (e.g., '2-3 gallons') without precision
No option to account for multiple coats or texture

keywords.serpWeakness: Top pages from calculators.net or omnicalculator are ad-heavy, slow on mobile, and don't support irregular rooms. No PWA or offline capability.
userComplaints: Can't draw or input L-shaped rooms
Doesn't adjust for porous surfaces like fresh drywall

keywords.serpWeakness: Most tools are from small blogs with dated UI and no metric unit toggle. Several are thin content with a single input field.
userComplaints: Only works in square feet, no metric option
Does not subtract windows and doors automatically

keywords.serpWeakness: Retailer calculators (Lowes, Home Depot) push product recommendations aggressively. Niche sites lack responsive design and are often unmaintained since 2019.
userComplaints: Pushes specific paint brands even when not relevant
Asks for email to see the estimate

keywords.serpWeakness: Many results are lead-gen pages for painting services, not self‑serve calculators. The remaining are generic affiliate sites with outdated JavaScript.
userComplaints: Calculator is actually a quote request form
Estimates don't include ceiling or trim paint

keywords.serpWeakness: Simple tools that only multiply width × height, ignoring multiple walls. Some are Flash-based or have no mobile touch support.
userComplaints: Can't input separate wall dimensions, only total area
No reset button, must reload page to start over

keywords.serpWeakness: SERP filled with manufacturer claims (350-400 sq ft) but no interactive tool to apply that to a real room. Opportunity to combine data with a calculator.
userComplaints: Confusing because coverage varies by paint type, no explanation
Doesn't connect coverage to actual room dimensions

keywords.serpWeakness: Home Depot and Lowe’s dominate with product‑heavy calculators that require brand selection. Independent sites lack pricing data and show ads that mislead.
userComplaints: Estimates only for their own paint brands
Doesn't include primer, tape, rollers etc.

keywords.serpWeakness: Comparison articles list the same big‑brand calculators without real testing. No page offers a single superior tool for power users.
userComplaints: Review sites just embed the same iframes, no original tool
No consideration for user experience or privacy

keywords.serpWeakness: Most tools assume rectangular rooms, fail with closets, vaulted ceilings, or arches. Mobile versions often break layout.
userComplaints: Can't handle sloped ceilings or alcoves
No way to save the calculation for later

keywords.serpWeakness: Sites like PaintCare and calculator.net have outdated SSL certificates or excessive pop‑ups. None offer a printable summary.
userComplaints: Requires too many steps to get a simple gallon count
Print layout breaks and wastes paper

keywords.serpWeakness: Small WordPress sites with abandoned plugins. Often not secure (no HTTPS) or error‑prone with JavaScript.
userComplaints: Gives significantly low estimates, not enough paint
No guidance on primer or number of coats included

keywords.serpWeakness: Almost no US-focused tool offers metric by default; metric users forced to convert, causing friction. Top results are EU sites with different can sizes.
userComplaints: Must convert meters to feet first, defeats purpose
Assumes gallon sizes that don't match local purchase

designConfig.voice: Creative, inspiring — like a professional painter sharing hard-won tips. Visual, concrete, excited about color.
designConfig.tagline: Know how much paint you need before you open a single can.
